youtube_items.title: [YT] Recycling Marine Plastic Waste A Circular Economy Case | Suzanna Laskaridis | TEDxAthens
youtube_items.summary: In 2017, I was approached by a team of designers to help finance a project they were doing here in Greece. The aim was to reuse old fishing nets and turn them into filament for 3D and turn them into filament for 3D printers. I thought the idea of recycling marine plastic waste into something workable was absolutely fantastic. So, I started working in my mind the idea of how this could actually be idea of how this could actually be reused. We put together the best minds in the fields of marine plastic waste, new technologies, and circular economy and dove right in. We have a storage area just outside of Athens, so we started suggesting to various friends and NGOs and various other companies who had been collecting marine plastic waste up to that point that we take them back to our storage t

youtube_items.title: [YT] Circular economy for plastics
youtube_items.summary: Since 1950 approximately 8.3 billion tons of plastics have been produced globally and 5-13 million tons of plastic waste seeps into our environment and oceans every year. The danish cleantech mechanical recycling company PLASTIX is specializing in converting plastic fibres primarily used fishnets, trawls and ropes that would previously have ended up in the ocean or landfill into high-quality and virgin-like Green Plastic raw materials. PLASTIX received a feasibility study grant from Nopef, a fund financed by the Nordic Council of Ministers and managed by Nefco to assess the South African market and, as a result, established a local subsidiary in 2017. Today, it obtains post-use discarded nets, trawls and other fishing gear from the South African market. PLASTIX is currently expanding its p

raw_items.title: EREF Auction Raises More Than $4 Million to Advance Waste and Recycling Research
raw_items.summary: The Environmental Research & Education Foundation (EREF) raised over $4 million during its 2026 Annual Charitable Auction, held during the inaugural Waste Leadership Summit in Washington, D.C.

raw_items.title: Casella’s Roadmap for Renewable Energy and Driving a Circular Economy
raw_items.summary: In this Waste360 Q&A, Casella CEO Ned Coletta catches us up to speed on facility projects and shares what his team learned from testing electric trucks in the real world. He takes us behind the scenes of some exciting partnerships, including work with the University of Vermont to advance a circular economy.

raw_items.summary: In this edition of the Business Report, we recap the highlights of the Investor Summit, as well as several of the education sessions from the Waste Leadership Summit.

raw_items.summary: Waste360 is proud to announce its fourth annual Women Who Inspire award winners. Our program, sponsored by Wastequip, recognizes incredible women who are making a difference across waste, recycling, and sustainability.

raw_items.title: The world’s plastic pollution crisis, explained | National Geographic
raw_items.summary: Why was plastic invented? Plastics made from fossil fuels are just over a century old. Belgian chemist Leo Baekeland created the first fully synthetic plastic in 1907.

raw_items.title: TONTOTON at UNOC3: A Certified Local Solution to the Global...
raw_items.summary: With global plastic waste export policies tightening, the urgency for local, effective, and scalable solutions has never been greater. TONTOTON's work is driven by a deep commitment to Cambodia's environmental future and the belief that lasting change is possible through collective action.

raw_items.title: Environmental and health implications of improper waste ...
raw_items.summary: While astounding cities promise employment and opportunities, they do not seem to be able to keep up with the magnifying concern of waste management. This research looks at the implications of poor waste management on the demography as well as on the environment’s state and public health.

raw_items.title: Environmental Consequences Of Poor Waste Disposal: A Growing ...
raw_items.summary: Oct 18, 2025 · Poor waste disposal devastates ecosystems, pollutes water, harms wildlife, and threatens human health. Learn about this growing environmental crisis.

raw_items.title: Hazardous waste management and one health approach - Frontiers
raw_items.summary: Oct 7, 2025 · In the context of solid waste management, the One Health approach highlights how improper handling of municipal, industrial, agricultural, or biomedical waste can simultaneously affect ecosystems, animals, and human populations.
